title: MorseHGP3D canonical data contract v2
description: Strict, versioned serialization contract for MorseHGP3D scientific and operational records. The root instance is a MorseHGP3DResult; every requested record type is independently available under $defs.

SchemaVersion.description: Semantic version of each independently serializable record in this contract.

MorseRole.description: Order-specific H0 role of a critical centre. For index one, arm_count=|U| and local_multiplicity=|U|-1; for index zero, arm_count=0 and multiplicity=1.

barycentric_signs.description: Signs in the fixed order of minimal_support_ids; this is a tuple and is not sorted.

CriticalEvent.description: Canonical critical sphere. event_id hashes the complete shell, interior, minimal support and exact witnesses, never an approximate centre or thread-arrival index.

PointLabel.description: Canonical point label: ascending unique zero-based point IDs.

GabrielHyperedge.description: One canonical H0 hyperedge emitted by a rank order+1 critical event. facet_point_ids contains every facet; strict_arm_point_ids contains only the pre-lot arms indexed by shell removals.

GammaCoface.description: One exhaustive Gamma relation. Exact hgp_reduced replay requires every order k+1 coface, including non-Gabriel relations that create only a silent incidence at their insertion level.

AttachmentStep.description: One causal, strictly descending locator step. Steps retain traversal order and are not canonically sorted as a set.

descent_path.description: Causal order from the arm to its target; it is not sorted. Empty for a direct Gabriel-flow attachment.

Attachment.description: Globally replayable arm attachment. Its identity key is exactly (event_id, order, removed_shell_id); attachment_id is the canonical hash of that tuple.

ComponentState.description: Canonical component snapshot immediately before or after one exact-level batch.

CoverageDelta.description: Replayable coverage growth for a batch, including q=1 growth that creates no forest node.

EqualLevelBatch.description: Simultaneous contraction at one exact squared level and order. gamma_coface_ids includes every exhaustive Gamma relation, so a replay batch may have no critical event. pre_lot_components are frozen strict-sublevel states; post_lot_components are states after all unions.

CanonicalId.description: Lower-case SHA-256 digest of the type-specific canonical identity projection.

PointId.description: Zero-based canonical point index, bounded to the exactly representable JSON integer range.

NullableOrder.description: A multicover order, or null when the corresponding H0 role does not exist in the computed tower.

Count.description: Exact non-negative count in the JSON safe-integer range.

PositiveCount.description: Exact positive count in the JSON safe-integer range.

SignedIntegerString.description: Canonical arbitrary-precision signed base-10 integer: no plus sign, no leading zero, and no negative zero.

Binary64Bits.description: Exactly sixteen lower-case hexadecimal digits containing one finite IEEE-754 binary64 bit pattern, without a 0x prefix.

CanonicalInputBinary64Bits.description: Finite binary64 input bit pattern after canonicalizing negative zero to positive zero.

ExactPositiveRational.description: Positive dimensionless rational in lowest terms with positive denominator.

SimilarityTransform.description: Explicit similarity applied before computation. When applied=false, scale must be one and translation must be zero by semantic validation.

InputSemantics.description: Normative interpretation of an input cloud. Coordinates are exact dyadics reconstructed from finite binary64 bits; public levels use squared input-coordinate units.

coordinate_bits.description: x, y, z binary64 bit patterns in that fixed axis order. This is a tuple, not a set.

CertifiedPoint3.description: Canonical finite input site. point_id is assigned only after exact coordinate canonicalization; source_indices preserve occurrence provenance.

ExactRational3.description: Canonical homogeneous rational triple (Cx,Cy,Cz,Dc): Dc>0 and gcd(|Cx|,|Cy|,|Cz|,Dc)=1.

ExactLevel.description: Canonical non-negative squared radius N/D: D>0 and gcd(N,D)=1. Equality and ordering use exact cross-products, never a floating tolerance.

approximation_bits.description: Approximate x, y, z tuple used only for proposal and diagnostics.

VertexWitness.description: Replayable exact cell-vertex witness. Approximate coordinates never certify incidence or closure.

MergeNode.description: Canonical birth or multifusion node. A birth has no children; a merge has at least two by semantic validation. No q=1 growth node is created.

MergeForest.description: One horizontal compressed hierarchy at a one-based order. Nodes are content-addressed and sorted; overlapping covered_point_ids across components are valid for k>=2.

VerticalAssignment.description: Image of one compressed source component in the adjacent lower-order forest at a fixed exact level.

VerticalMap.description: Adjacent vertical map from source_order=k+1 to target_order=k. Semantic validation requires source_order=target_order+1 and zero failures for an exact run.

PartialLocus.description: Canonical unresolved locus that bounds, rather than hides, an incomplete result.

FragmentHint.description: Non-certifying discovery fragment. It can seed canonical reconstruction but never substitutes for a closed CanonicalCellCertificate.

CanonicalCellCertificate.description: Certificate for the canonical reconstruction of C_depth(label) intersected with the padded box. Exact closure requires every boolean completeness flag and status=closed.

PartialScope.description: Explicit certified perimeter of a partial or event-only result. Open depths, orders and loci cannot be inferred closed by omission.

NullableCount.description: A non-negative safe integer or an explicitly unlimited/null quantity.

NullableSeconds.description: A non-negative duration in SI seconds or an explicitly unlimited/null duration.

BudgetPolicy.description: Typed resource policy. null means unbounded by this policy, never unknown. A budget cannot silently downgrade require_exact=true.

BudgetSnapshot.description: Transactional budget accounting at a deterministic processing boundary. remaining is null exactly when its limit is null.

CheckpointArtifact.description: One checksummed durable checkpoint artifact. Paths are relative and may not be interpreted before application-level traversal checks.

CheckpointManifest.description: Atomically published, checksummed restart manifest. A partially applied equal-level batch is never publishable.

Backend.description: Implementation backend that performed the computation.

Profile.description: Requested or effective mathematical output profile.

ExecutionMode.description: Closure policy requested by the caller.

PublicStatus.description: Actual scientific status obtained by one run, distinct from backend, profile, mode and forest semantics.

PredicateCounts.description: Counts of numerical proposals and exact certification stages; all fields are dimensionless call counts.

FallbackCounts.description: Dimensionless counts of explicitly handled fallback and failure paths.

WorkMemoryCounters.description: Work counts are dimensionless; every field ending in _bytes uses exact bytes, never MiB or MB.

SoftwareEnvironment.description: Reproducibility manifest for the code and hardware that produced a certificate.

reconstruction_contract_id.description: hgp_reduced invokes the v2 K-polyhedra contract; full_pi0 invokes M.1. The two identifiers are never interchanged.

proof_basis.description: Scientific basis actually available to this run. gamma_exhaustive_reference is the only exact hgp_reduced basis and is restricted to reference_cpu. gabriel_positive_connectivity and m1_conditional_contract provide conditional partial-refinement evidence only; active v2 has no exact full_pi0 basis.

catalog_complete_by_rank.description: Positional array: offset r-1 records completeness of closed rank r.

attachments_complete_by_order.description: Positional array: offset k-1 records attachment completeness at one-based order k.

gamma_complete_by_order.description: Positional array: offset k-1 records exhaustive enumeration and batch assignment of every Gamma facet and coface at one-based order k.

batches_complete_by_order.description: Positional array: offset k-1 records exact-level batch completeness at one-based order k.

RunCertificate.description: Fail-closed evidence for one run. A status exact is a semantic function of profile/proof basis, every completeness field, zero remaining unknowns/failures, and stop_reason=completed; benchmark quality never changes it.

ComponentPointSet.description: Optional materialization of the observation union covered by one forest node; sets from distinct nodes may overlap.

BenchmarkTiming.description: One raw monotonic-clock benchmark sample in SI seconds; phases may overlap and therefore need not sum to total_s.

BenchmarkResources.description: Raw resource measurements: bytes, watts and degrees Celsius as named; null telemetry means unavailable, not zero.

GcpSafetyRecord.description: Target-specific GCP safety evidence. When used=true, semantic validation requires the guarded G4 Spot/STOP values, 30..28800 seconds, both cut-offs and final_state=TERMINATED before handoff.

BenchmarkRecord.description: One raw benchmark repetition. Aggregate p50/p95/p99 are derived records, never substituted for raw samples; clustering quality cannot change public_status.

embedded_input_points.description: Optional fixture/replay payload; large production results normally omit it and rely on input_sha256.

MorseHGP3DResult.description: Canonical MorseHGP3D output. Exact/partial hierarchy results carry forest_semantics; verified_events_only omits it and carries partial_scope. Arrays remain present and empty when that record class has none.
